title: The Missense Mutant ClC-5 (E211A), associated with Proton/Chloride Uncoupling Exacerbates Renal Pathology Compared to the ClC-5 null Mutant.
abstract: Dent disease type 1 is an X-linked proximal tubulopathy caused by mutations in CLCN5, which encodes the chloride/proton exchanger ClC-5. While loss-of-function mutations impair endocytosis and lead to progressive renal disease, the pathological consequences of uncoupling mutations that selectively disrupt Cl-/H+ exchange remain incompletely understood.
